title: Setting sigstore to a normal path (without file://) causes a segmentation fault SIGSEGV
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

If I configure the `sigstore` to be a path without `file://`, podman segfaults.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Set up podman to read image signatures from a regular filesystem path. `~/.config/containers/registries.d/default.yaml`:
    ```yml
    default-docker:
      sigstore: /home/faern/podman-sigstore/  # <- causes the SIGSEGV
      # sigstore: file:///home/faern/podman-sigstore/ # <- this works
    ```

2. Pull any image: `podman pull alpine`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ podman pull alpine
....
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...

[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x0 pc=0x556b6c054083]

....
```

I'm not including the full stacktrace. The remaining goroutines seems to be mostly GC.


**Describe the results you expected:**

Since podman can read the sigstore from file, I would expect it to be able to take regular file paths. But even if the `file://` prefix is a hard requirement, the command should not crash in this way, rather print that the configuration is invalid.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Tested latest version: No. But I can't find any reported issue or posted PR on this issue. So I would be surprised if the bug was not still there in 4.3.0
Checked the troubleshooting guide: Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

My physical machine, running Fedora 36

comments.body: Thanks for reporting this! It will be fixed by https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1704.
